---
title: "Intermediate Elective: Birds"
author: "Kim Wong"
format: pdf
execute: 
  message: false
  warning: false 
---

# Set up

## Packages 
```{r}
#| label: packages

# general use 
library(tidyverse)

# file organization 
library(here)

# data visualization
library(lubridate)
library(showtext)
library(camcorder)
library(ggtext)
library(glue)
library(tsibble)
library(rcartocolor)
library(paletteer)

```

## Data

```{r}
birds <- read_csv(here("data", "birds-copy.csv"))
```


# Problems 

## 1. Explain your inspiration

I chose the UFO Sightings visualization for inspiration. I want to work with the birds dataset, and I think I could show bird counts by season with a visualization like this. When I was looking through all the options, I was looking for something that could display 4 variables (for me: time, count, season, and eBird group), and this one does that clearly. For my visualization, time and bird count will be the axes, seasons will be the colors, and each plot will be for an eBird group. I am picking eBird groups based on some of the birds I've seen at NCOS these past few weeks!

## 2. Plan your figure 

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("figure-sketch.png", error = FALSE)
```

## 3. Code your figure

```{r}
#| label: cleaning-data

# cleaning birds data
birds_clean <- birds |> 
  filter(e_bird_group %in% c("Hummingbirds",
                             "Vultures, Hawks, and Allies",
                             "Gulls, Terns, and Skimmers",
                             "Woodpeckers",
                             "Shorebirds",
                             "Tyrant Flycatchers: Pewees, Kingbirds, and Allies",
                             "Waterfowl",
                             "Jays, Magpies, Crows, and Ravens",
                             "Thrushes"),
         repeat_observation == "No") |> 
  mutate(year = year(observation_date)) |> 
  select(e_bird_group, year, season, count) |> 
  mutate(season = as.factor(season)) |> 
  mutate(season = fct_relevel(season, "Fall", "Winter", "Spring", "Summer"),
         season = fct_rev(season))
```

```{r}
# colors
bg_col <- "#fafafa"
col_pal <- rcartocolor::carto_pal(n = 5, "Sunset")
dark_col <- "grey20"

# load fonts
font_add_google("Ubuntu", "Ubuntu")
showtext_auto()

# text
title <- "Bird abundance by season at NCOS"

st <- glue::glue("This figure shows the percent of total bird counts per season for nine eBird groups at North Campus Space. For some groups, the counts are pretty even across all seasons. For others, the count appears to be higher in <span style='color:{col_pal[1]}'>summer</span> and <span style='color:{col_pal[2]}'>spring</span>. Some have low counts in <span style='color:{col_pal[4]}'>winter</span>. <span style='color:{col_pal[5]}'>Fall</span> counts seem mostly proportional.")
tg <- paste0(
  "<span style='font-size:38pt; font-weight: bold;'>**UFO Sightings**</span><br><br>", st,
  "<br><br>**Data**: North Campus Open Space<br><br>"
)
```



```{r}
#| label: plot

ggplot(
  data = birds_clean,
  mapping = aes(x = year, y = count, fill = season)
) +
  geom_area(position = "fill") +
  geom_text(
    mapping = aes(x = 2021, y = 0.5, label = e_bird_group),
    colour = bg_col,
    family = "Ubuntu",
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(2017, 2025)) +
  facet_wrap(~e_bird_group, nrow = 3, ncol = 3) +
  scale_fill_carto_d(palette = "Sunset") +
  labs(
    x = "",
    y = "",
    tag = tg
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.background = element_rect(
      fill = bg_col,
      colour = bg_col
    )) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12, base_family = "Ubuntu") +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.background = element_rect(
      fill = bg_col,
      colour = bg_col
    ),
    panel.background = element_rect(
      fill = bg_col,
      colour = bg_col
    ),
    plot.tag = element_textbox_simple(
      lineheight = 0.5,
      colour = dark_col,
      margin = margin(b = 40),
      maxwidth = 0.2,
      halign = 0,
      size = 18
    ),
    plot.tag.position = c(-0.1, 0.5),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = -10)),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0, "lines"),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 15, 10, 200),
  )
```
